description: This figure presents double-logarithmic plots of true stress (lnσ) versus true strain (lnε) for Fe-20Mn-6Al-0.6C-0.15Si austenitic low-density steel at three temperatures: room temperature (RT, gray squares), -77°C (red circles), and -196°C (blue triangles). The data is fitted with bilinear segments to determine the work hardening exponents (n) for two distinct deformation stages. At RT, the values are n₁=0.174 and n₂=0.362; at -77°C, n₁=0.181 and n₂=0.439; at -196°C, n₁=0.204 and n₂=0.448. The results demonstrate that both the initial and secondary work hardening rates increase significantly as temperature decreases, with n₂ showing a more pronounced temperature sensitivity. This enhanced work hardening capacity at cryogenic temperatures correlates with the activation of mechanical twinning and increased dislocation accumulation, contributing to the superior strength-ductility synergy observed in this steel for cryogenic applications.
